#[derive(Debug)]
enum ParseError {
    InvalidFormat,
    OutOfRange,
    MissingField,
}

fn parse_coordinate(input: &str) -> Result<(f64, f64), ParseError> {
    // GOOD: error strings built only when error occurs, using match arms
    let parts: Vec<&str> = input.split(',').collect();
    if parts.len() != 2 {
        return Err(ParseError::InvalidFormat);
    }
    let x: f64 = parts[0].parse().map_err(|_| ParseError::InvalidFormat)?;
    let y: f64 = parts[1].parse().map_err(|_| ParseError::InvalidFormat)?;
    Ok((x, y))
}

impl std::fmt::Display for ParseError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            ParseError::InvalidFormat => write!(f, "invalid coordinate format, expected 'x,y'"),
            ParseError::OutOfRange => write!(f, "coordinate value out of range"),
            ParseError::MissingField => write!(f, "missing required field"),
        }
    }
}
